title: Hebe amplexicaulis
taxonomy: Hebe amplexicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The classification of Hebe amplexicaulis is as a member of the genus Hebe, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the 'New Zealand verbenas'. Hebe amplexicaulis is a type of evergreen shrub. This species is known for its small, white flowers and its compact, rounded growth habit.

intro: Hebe amplexicaulis, also known as New Zealand hebe, is a flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hebe amplexicaulis include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
